column11:
Quantum simulation is dominated by linear algebra operations, primarily structured matrix-vector multiplications. These operations exhibit a high degree of data parallelism, which makes them suitable for multithreaded and GPU exe- cution. Furthermore, the regular structure of the quantum gates allows the matrix operations to be implemented implicitly, avoiding the explicit construction of large matrices [5]. Also, the method requires limited CPU-GPU communication, with the CPU mainly responsible for kernel dispatch. Most high-performance quantum simulators target data- center GPUs with high memory bandwidth, large core counts, and fast interconnects such as NVLink, enabling multi-GPU simulations of larger systems [6].
